nodes.name: [BH] Log Inbound Message

body.jsonOutput: ={{ JSON.stringify({ contact_id: $('node-ig-bh1').first().json[0]?.id, channel: 'instagram', direction: 'inbound', content: $('Extract IG Message').first().json.text, metadata: { igSenderId: $('Extract IG Message').first().json.igSenderId, mid: $('Extract IG Message').first().json.mid, handledBy: 'manual' }, ai_handled: false }) }}

nodes._notes: Business hours — team replies manually via Meta Business Suite or WATI if connected. n8n just logs.

body.jsonOutput:
={{ JSON.stringify({ model: 'gpt-4o', messages: [{ role: 'system', content: 'You are Aria, a warm and professional AI receptionist for a Hong Kong med-aesthetics clinic. You are responding to Instagram DMs.

Guidelines:
- Respond naturally in the same language the client uses (English or Traditional Chinese)
- Instagram DMs should feel conversational and friendly, not overly formal
- Keep replies under 200 characters where possible — this is DM, not email
- Never quote prices; say the team will confirm during consultation
- If the client wants to book or shows strong interest, extract their intent using this exact pattern:
[BOOKING_INTENT]{"name":"<name or unknown>","service":"<service>","preferred_time":"<time or empty>"}[/BOOKING_INTENT]
- Clinic hours: Mon–Sat 9am–7pm HKT. You are handling after-hours messages.' }, ...$json.openAiMessages], max_tokens: 350, temperature: 0.65 }) }}
={{ JSON.stringify({ contact_id: $json.contactId, channel: 'instagram', history: $json.finalHistory, last_message_at: new Date().toISOString(), message_count: $json.finalMessageCount }) }}

nodes.name: [AH] Send IG Reply
nodes._notes: Page Access Token must have instagram_manage_messages permission. Token must be refreshed every 60 days unless using a System User token (permanent).

nodes.name: [AH] Batch Log to Supabase
nodes._notes: n8n will call this node once per item from Log Messages — two HTTP calls, one for each message direction.

body.jsonOutput: ={{ JSON.stringify({ contact_id: $('node-ig-ah5').first().json.contactId, status: 'consultation_requested', channel: 'instagram', service_interest: $('node-ig-ah5').first().json.bookingIntent?.service || 'Unspecified', preferred_time: $('node-ig-ah5').first().json.bookingIntent?.preferred_time || '', booking_data: $('node-ig-ah5').first().json.bookingIntent || {}, notes: 'Captured after hours via Instagram DM AI' }) }}

={{ JSON.stringify({ text: ':camera: *New booking intent captured (Instagram DM — after hours)*
*Service:* ' + ($('node-ig-ah5').first().json.bookingIntent?.service || 'Not specified') + '
*Preferred time:* ' + ($('node-ig-ah5').first().json.bookingIntent?.preferred_time || 'Not specified') + '
_Reply via Meta Business Suite or tag team to follow up_' }) }}

_meta.notes: SETUP CHECKLIST: (1) In Meta Developer Console: add your n8n webhook URL (both GET and POST go to the same path 'instagram-webhook') — set Verify Token to 'scalar_verify_2025'. (2) Grant instagram_manage_messages permission to your Meta app. (3) Replace YOUR_META_PAGE_ACCESS_TOKEN — generate a long-lived Page Access Token or use a System User token. (4) Replace YOUR_SUPABASE_SERVICE_KEY in all Authorization header values. (5) Replace YOUR_SLACK_WEBHOOK_URL. (6) Two webhook nodes (GET + POST) share the same path — n8n routes by method automatically.
